titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Elliot to Edward Kerr, 21 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Elliot

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dairie's
Drumcore
Enniskillen 21st March, 1916. Dear MrKerr The gate you refer to in your letter of the
17th was put on without the knowledge or authority
of the late owners of Ballygonnill, M rT Elliot, and as
there never was a cart road through that field
I had the gate removed to where it now his
and will raise no objection if you put
it on where it now is for your own convenience
or that of your caretaker. I understand there is
a pass that way into your farm inRabionfor
carrying turf and luggage of any kind, But if you
wish to put on a gate and cart that way, it will
be alright. I have an objection to the making
of a cart road through my meadow field where
I intend to plough as soon as possible. When we get settled in Ballygonnill if you
run up we can have a look at the place
and a chat over it. In the meantime
I remain
Yours Faithfully Thos. Elliott
